EDITORIAL.

——————

We wonder what will be the next move
in regard to the Croke Memorial dispute.
Ts it too much to suppose that the Thurles
Urban Council will revoke their previous
decision refusing a site for this £1,000
Memorial Statue? Certainly, that body
would Appear-to be past counsel if they
still continue to" obstruct the unanimous
wish of the Gaelic public, who made the
interests of Thurles the supreme considera-
tion in as bitter a controversy as we wish
to see. Several bodies, both Gaelic and
Local Govermental, bave placed on record
their non-acquiescence in the propriety of
the action of the Thurles body. We have
yet to see one motion sigailying approval
adopted by any representative body, Surely
this large bulk of opinion, tending in one
single direction, ought to give pause to
these detestors of congestion, in whose
locality an extra £1,000 statue is more of
fan embarrasment than an addition. We
venture to say there is not another town in
Ireland but would feel honoured by the
offer of such an opportunity of beautifying
their neighbourhood, at no personal cost oF
